h1. SBOM Pipeline & Vulnerability Management with Dependency-Track
{info:title=Document Information}
*Document Owner:* Platform Engineering Team | *Last Updated:* 23 April 2026 | *Status:* {color:green}*ACTIVE*{color}
{info}

This page describes the end-to-end Software Bill of Materials (SBOM) pipeline that automatically generates, uploads, and tracks vulnerabilities across all managed repositories using [Dependency-Track|https://dependencytrack.org].

{toc:maxLevel=3|style=disc|minLevel=2}


----


h2. What is an SBOM?
A *Software Bill of Materials (SBOM)* is a formal, machine-readable inventory of every component, library, and dependency within a software product — along with their versions, licences, and known vulnerabilities.

* *Transparency* — know exactly what is inside every artefact
* *Compliance* — satisfy regulatory and supply-chain audit requirements
* *Risk Reduction* — identify and remediate vulnerabilities proactively
* *Licence Governance* — detect licence conflicts before they reach production
* *Faster Response* — immediately know which projects are affected when a new CVE is published

{note:title=SBOM Standard}
The [CycloneDX|https://cyclonedx.org] format is used throughout this pipeline (JSON serialisation). CycloneDX is an OWASP standard and is natively supported by Dependency-Track.
{note}


----


h2. SBOM Generation
h3. Maven Projects
The [CycloneDX Maven Plugin|https://github.com/CycloneDX/cyclonedx-maven-plugin] is added to the project POM. It runs automatically during the *package* phase and outputs a CycloneDX JSON SBOM at {{target/bom.json}}.

{code:language=xml|title=pom.xml — CycloneDX Maven Plugin}
<plugin>
    <groupId>org.cyclonedx</groupId>
    <artifactId>cyclonedx-maven-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>2.9.1</version>
    <configuration>
        <projectType>library</projectType>
        <schemaVersion>1.4</schemaVersion>
        <includeBomSerialNumber>true</includeBomSerialNumber>
        <includeCompileScope>true</includeCompileScope>
        <includeTestScope>false</includeTestScope>
        <includeRuntimeScope>true</includeRuntimeScope>
        <includeSystemScope>true</includeSystemScope>
        <includeProvidedScope>true</includeProvidedScope>
        <includeLicenseText>false</includeLicenseText>
        <outputFormat>all</outputFormat>
    </configuration>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <phase>package</phase>
            <goals>
                <goal>makeAggregateBom</goal>
            </goals>
        </execution>
    </executions>
</plugin>
{code}

h3. Non-Maven Projects (cdxgen)
For Node.js, Python, Go, and other ecosystems the [cdxgen|https://github.com/CycloneDX/cdxgen] tool runs inside a Docker container as a Jenkins pipeline step — no installation required.

{code:language=groovy|title=Jenkinsfile — cdxgenRepo shared library step}
// Jenkinsfile (non-Maven project)
cdxgenRepo(
    projectName: "my-nodejs-app",
    projectVersion: "1.0.0",
    dtUrl: "https://dependencytrack.example.com",
    dtApiKey: credentials('DT_API_KEY')
)
{code}

{tip:title=Multi-Language Support}
cdxgen supports over 20 package ecosystems including npm, pip, Gradle, Go modules, Cargo, and more. The Jenkins shared library ({{vars/cdxgenRepo.groovy}}) abstracts the Docker invocation.
{tip}

h3. Docker / Container Images (Syft + Grype)
*Syft* (Anchore) scans Docker image layers to generate a container SBOM, capturing every OS package and language library baked into the image. *Grype* (also Anchore) consumes the Syft SBOM for immediate vulnerability triage.

* Scans a pulled or locally built Docker image via the {{syftImage()}} shared-library step
* Identifies OS packages (Alpine/Debian/RHEL) *and* language libraries inside the image
* Outputs CycloneDX JSON — uploads to Dependency-Track via {{uploadSBOM()}}
* Run {{grypeScan()}} immediately after to get a pre-upload vulnerability report

h3. SBOM Generation Tools — Summary
||Tool||Type||Use Case||Jenkins Step||
|*cdxgen*|SBOM Generator|Polyglot — Java, Node.js, Python, Go, .NET, Ruby, Cargo & more|{{cdxgenRepo()}}|
|*CycloneDX Maven Plugin*|SBOM Generator|Java/Maven — exact resolved-dependency-tree SBOM at build time|Auto via {{mvn package}}|
|*Syft (Anchore)*|SBOM Generator|Docker/container images — scans image layers|{{syftImage()}}|
|*Grype (Anchore)*|Vuln Scanner|Consumes a Syft/cdxgen SBOM; pre-upload vulnerability triage|{{grypeScan()}}|
|*CycloneDX Gradle/Node/.NET*|SBOM Generator|Ecosystem-specific build plugins for Gradle, npm, and .NET SDK|Language-native|


----


h2. Dependency-Track Overview
!https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DependencyTrack/dependency-track/master/docs/images/screenshots/dashboard.png|width=700,alt=Dependency-Track Portfolio Dashboard!

Dependency-Track is an OWASP flagship project that provides a continuous component analysis platform. Once an SBOM is uploaded it:

# Parses every component and resolves it against its package ecosystem
# Queries vulnerability intelligence sources (NVD, OSV, GitHub Advisories, VulnDB)
# Calculates inherited risk for each project and component
# Surfaces findings in a searchable, filterable dashboard
# Triggers policy violations for licence and version rules

!https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DependencyTrack/dependency-track/master/docs/images/screenshots/collection-projects-details.png|width=700,alt=Collection Project Detail!

h3. Key Capabilities
||Capability||Description||
|Portfolio Dashboard|Aggregate risk view across all projects|
|Policy Engine|Enforce licence, severity, and CVSS threshold rules|
|VEX / Analysis|Record analyst decisions against each finding|
|Notifications|Webhook, e-mail, and Slack alerts on new findings|
|REST API|Full programmatic access — CI/CD and SIEM integration|

h3. Vulnerability Intelligence Sources
Dependency-Track queries the following feeds automatically. New vulnerabilities are matched against your entire component inventory the moment a feed refreshes — no manual CVE searching required.

||Source||Maintained By||Scope||Notes||
|*NVD* — National Vulnerability Database|NIST|All languages & ecosystems|Canonical CVE registry with CVSS v2/v3/v4. Primary authoritative source.|
|*OSV* — Open Source Vulnerabilities|Google|PyPI, npm, Maven, Go, Cargo, Gems|Open DB with precise package-URL matching; often earlier disclosure than NVD.|
|*GitHub Advisory Database*|GitHub / community|GitHub-hosted packages|Curated advisories tied directly to package versions; highly accurate matching.|
|*Sonatype OSS Index*|Sonatype|Maven Central, npm|Component intelligence with licence metadata; strong Java coverage.|
|*VulnDB*|Risk Based Security|All ecosystems|Optional commercial feed — broader coverage and earlier disclosure than NVD.|

{info:title=Automatic Feed Refresh}
All feeds are queried automatically by Dependency-Track on a rolling schedule. You can also trigger an immediate re-analysis via the UI or REST API ({{PUT /api/v1/project/<uuid>/metrics/current}}) after a feed update.
{info}


----


h2. Vulnerability Triage Workflow
!https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DependencyTrack/dependency-track/master/docs/images/screenshots/vulnerabilities.png|width=700,alt=Vulnerability Findings List!

When a new SBOM is uploaded Dependency-Track immediately analyses all components and surfaces findings. The recommended triage process:

# *Review* — open the Findings tab for the project and review by severity (Critical first)
# *Investigate* — click a finding to see CVE details, CVSS vector, affected versions, and fix version
# *Analyse* — record an analysis state and justification (see below)
# *Remediate or Suppress* — update the dependency version or suppress with documented reason
# *Verify* — re-run the pipeline; the finding should clear once the fixed version is uploaded

h3. Analysis States
||State||Colour||Meaning||
|{color:green}*NOT_AFFECTED*{color}|Green|Component is present but the vulnerability does not apply in this context|
|{color:blue}*IN_TRIAGE*{color}|Blue|Under active investigation — do not suppress yet|
|{color:red}*EXPLOITABLE*{color}|Red|Confirmed exploitable — remediation required|
|{color:darkorange}*FALSE_POSITIVE*{color}|Yellow|Incorrect match by the scanner — documented and suppressed|
|{color:gray}*NOT_SET*{color}|Grey|Default — no analysis recorded yet|

{note:title=Audit Trail}
All state changes are immutable and timestamped. The full audit trail is available via the REST API and visible in the Finding detail panel.
{note}


----


h2. Recording Analysis & Comments
!https://raw.githubusercontent.com/DependencyTrack/dependency-track/master/docs/images/screenshots/audit-finding-project.png|width=700,alt=Audit Finding Panel!

The Analysis panel in Dependency-Track lets each team record a *structured* decision against every finding. This replaces informal spreadsheet tracking with a searchable, API-queryable audit log.

h3. Analysis Fields
* *State* — one of: NOT_AFFECTED, IN_TRIAGE, EXPLOITABLE, FALSE_POSITIVE
* *Justification* — controlled vocabulary: CODE_NOT_REACHABLE, PROTECTED_BY_MITIGATING_CONTROL, etc.
* *Response* — WILL_NOT_FIX, UPDATE, ROLLBACK, WORKAROUND_AVAILABLE, etc.
* *Detail* — free-text comment with full context (who, what, why, when)
* *Suppressed* — hides the finding from the active list once analysed

h3. Example Analysis Comment
{expand:title=Show example comment}
{code:language=none|title=Example analyst comment}
CVSS 9.8 — dependency is included in the WAR but the vulnerable endpoint (/admin/actuator) is blocked at the network perimeter (WAF rule WAF-042). Risk accepted pending upgrade to 3.2.1 in Q3 sprint. Owner: platform-team@example.com
{code}
{expand}

{tip:title=Analysis Propagation}
All comments and state changes are propagated to *all* projects that share the same component version — so you only need to analyse once.
{tip}


----


h2. Reporting
The Jenkins pipeline generates two PDF reports on every build:

h3. Security Analysis Report
Lists all current findings from Dependency-Track with analysis state, suppression flag, and analyst comments. Grouped by project and severity.

Sample findings:

||CVE||Severity||Component||Version||Fix Version||Status||
|CVE-2021-44228|{color:red}*CRITICAL*{color}|log4j-core|2.14.1|2.17.1|{color:green}*PATCHED*{color}|
|CVE-2022-42003|{color:darkorange}*HIGH*{color}|jackson-databind|2.13.2|2.13.4.1|{color:blue}*IN_TRIAGE*{color}|
|CVE-2023-34062|{color:darkorange}*HIGH*{color}|reactor-netty-http|1.0.38|1.0.39|{color:green}*NOT_AFFECTED*{color}|

h3. SBOM Component Report
A full inventory of every component, its version, package URL, and applicable licences — suitable for regulatory submissions and supply-chain audits.

{note:title=Report Archiving}
Both reports are archived as Jenkins build artefacts and can be downloaded from the build history at any time.
{note}


----


h2. CI/CD Pipeline Integration
SBOM generation and upload happens automatically on every Jenkins build. The pipeline shared library ({{vars/}}) provides reusable Groovy steps that can be dropped into any Jenkinsfile.

h3. Shared Library Steps
||Library Step||File||Purpose||
|{{uploadSBOM}}|{{vars/uploadSBOM.groovy}}|PUT project, resolve UUID, POST BOM to {{/api/v1/bom}}|
|{{cdxgenRepo}}|{{vars/cdxgenRepo.groovy}}|Run cdxgen in Docker; supports Java-specific image variant|
|{{createDTCollectionProject}}|{{vars/createDTCollectionProject.groovy}}|Create or update a collection (parent) project with child aggregation|
|{{exportDTReport}}|{{vars/exportDTReport.groovy}}|Download findings report for a single project via REST API|
|{{exportAllDTReports}}|{{vars/exportAllDTReports.groovy}}|Iterate all DT projects and export findings in bulk|
|{{fetchAllDTProjects}}|{{vars/fetchAllDTProjects.groovy}}|Return all project UUIDs from the DT portfolio|
|{{grypeScan}}|{{vars/grypeScan.groovy}}|Run Grype OCI image scan and upload results to DT|
|{{syftImage}}|{{vars/syftImage.groovy}}|Generate SBOM from a Docker image layer using Syft|
|{{listJenkinsPlugins}}|{{vars/listJenkinsPlugins.groovy}}|Export Jenkins plugin list as a CycloneDX SBOM|
|{{uploadPluginSBOMs}}|{{vars/uploadPluginSBOMs.groovy}}|Batch-upload plugin SBOMs to Dependency-Track|

h3. Example Jenkinsfile
{code:language=groovy|title=Jenkinsfile.java — Maven + Dependency-Track upload}
// Jenkinsfile.java — upload SBOM after Maven build
pipeline {
    stages {
        stage('Build & SBOM') {
            steps {
                sh 'mvn package -DskipTests'
                uploadSBOM(
                    projectName: env.JOB_BASE_NAME,
                    projectVersion: env.BUILD_NUMBER,
                    bomPath: 'target/bom.json',
                    dtUrl: 'https://dependencytrack.example.com',
                    dtApiKey: credentials('DT_API_KEY')
                )
            }
        }
    }
}
{code}

{info:title=Auto-Create Projects}
The {{uploadSBOM}} step automatically creates the project in Dependency-Track if it does not already exist, using a PUT/PATCH sequence followed by a POST to {{/api/v1/bom}}.
{info}


----


h2. API Explorer — Swagger UI
Dependency-Track ships with a built-in *Swagger / OpenAPI UI* that gives you an interactive, browser-based explorer for every endpoint the platform exposes. It is the fastest way to understand what is possible and to prototype integrations before writing code.

{info:title=Accessing the Swagger UI}
Open {{http://<your-dt-host>/api/swagger-ui/}} in a browser. Click *Authorize* in the top-right, enter your API key (Administration → Access Management → API Keys), and every endpoint becomes executable directly from the browser.
{info}

h3. Endpoint Groups
||Endpoint Group||Path Prefix||Key Operations||
|*BOM / SBOM Upload*|{{/api/v1/bom}}|Upload a CycloneDX or SPDX BOM; poll processing status|
|*Projects*|{{/api/v1/project}}|Create, update, delete, tag, and search projects; manage parent/child relationships|
|*Findings / Vulnerabilities*|{{/api/v1/finding}}|List findings for a project; export as FPF or SARIF; severity summary counts|
|*Analysis*|{{/api/v1/analysis}}|Read and write analysis state, justification, comments, suppression|
|*Vulnerability*|{{/api/v1/vulnerability}}|Look up a specific CVE; list vulns per component; feed status|
|*Component*|{{/api/v1/component}}|Search components; resolve package URLs; find all affected projects|
|*Policies*|{{/api/v1/policy}}|Create licence and severity policies; list violations|
|*Notifications*|{{/api/v1/notification}}|Configure Slack, e-mail, webhook, and MS Teams alerts|
|*Metrics*|{{/api/v1/metrics}}|Portfolio and per-project risk scores; trend data over time|

h3. Practical Uses of the Swagger UI
* *Prototype automations* — try an endpoint interactively before writing a Jenkins step or script
* *Debug uploads* — POST a BOM directly and inspect the raw JSON response
* *Bulk triage* — use {{PUT /api/v1/analysis}} to set analysis state across many findings from a script
* *Build dashboards* — {{GET /api/v1/metrics/portfolio}} returns live counts suitable for Grafana or a status page
* *Impact analysis* — {{GET /api/v1/component/identity}} with a package URL instantly shows every affected project
* *Policy automation* — create licence policies via {{POST /api/v1/policy}} and wire them to break-build notifications

{tip:title=OpenAPI Spec in this Repository}
The full OpenAPI specification ({{dependencytrack-openapi.yaml}}) is also available in this repository. Import it into Postman, Insomnia, or any REST client for a complete offline API reference with saved example requests.
{tip}

{note:title=Everything in the UI is in the API}
All pipeline operations in this project — SBOM upload, project creation, findings export, analysis recording — use the same REST API the Swagger UI exposes. If something is in the UI it can also be automated.
{note}

h3. Integration Ecosystem
The Dependency-Track REST API enables integration with ticketing systems (Jira), SIEMs (Splunk, Elasticsearch), and executive dashboards — giving full visibility from developer laptop to boardroom. Any tool that can make an HTTP request can integrate with Dependency-Track.

||Integration||API Endpoint(s) Used||What It Enables||
|*Jira / Ticketing*|{{GET /api/v1/finding/<project>}} + {{POST /api/v1/analysis}}|Auto-create Jira issues for Critical/High findings; sync analysis state back when tickets are resolved|
|*Splunk / Elasticsearch*|{{GET /api/v1/finding/<project>}} + notification webhooks|Stream vulnerability events and analysis decisions into your SIEM for correlation, alerting, and compliance reporting|
|*Grafana Dashboards*|{{GET /api/v1/metrics/portfolio}} + {{GET /api/v1/metrics/project/<uuid>}}|Pull live risk scores and trend data into Grafana panels for real-time executive-level visibility|
|*MS Teams / Slack*|{{POST /api/v1/notification/publisher}}|Configure webhook notifications for new Critical or High findings so the right team is alerted immediately|
|*PagerDuty / OpsGenie*|Outbound webhook from notification rules|Trigger on-call alerts when newly introduced Critical vulnerabilities are detected in production-bound builds|
|*Custom / Executive Dashboards*|{{GET /api/v1/metrics/portfolio}} + {{GET /api/v1/project}}|Schedule daily metrics exports; feed bespoke reporting tools or management dashboards with live risk data|

{info:title=Built-in Notification Channels}
Dependency-Track also supports outbound notifications via *Slack, MS Teams, e-mail, and generic webhooks* — all configurable from Administration → Notifications. Each notification can be scoped to specific projects, portfolios, or severity levels so the right team gets exactly the signal they need.
{info}


----


h2. Benefits Summary
Adopting the SBOM pipeline and Dependency-Track gives engineering, security, and compliance teams a single, continuous view of software risk — with no manual overhead.

||Area||Benefit||What it means in practice||
|(/) *Visibility*|*Complete Inventory*|Every direct and transitive dependency is known. No blind spots in the supply chain.|
|(!) *Security*|*Continuous Monitoring*|New CVEs are matched automatically — no manual scans. Alerts arrive the moment a feed refreshes.|
|(/) *Process*|*Structured Triage*|Analysis states, comments, and suppression give every security decision a governed, auditable home.|
|(/) *Efficiency*|*Automated Reporting*|PDF vulnerability and SBOM reports generated on every build. No manual export effort.|
|(!) *Engineering*|*Shift-Left Security*|SBOMs upload at build time — vulnerabilities surface during development, not after release.|
|(x) *Compliance*|*Policy Enforcement*|Severity and licence rules break the build before bad dependencies reach a release artefact.|
|(/) *Coverage*|*Polyglot Support*|Maven, Node.js, Python, Go, Docker images — one Jenkins shared-library step covers all ecosystems.|
|(/) *Integration*|*REST API*|Full headless API enables Jira, Grafana, Splunk, and custom tooling integrations.|
|(/) *Audit*|*Immutable Audit Trail*|Every state change, comment, and suppression is timestamped and attributed. Compliance evidence as a byproduct.|

{tip:title=Integration Ecosystem}
The Dependency-Track REST API enables integration with Jira, Splunk, Grafana, and executive dashboards — full visibility from developer laptop to boardroom.
{tip}

----

_Generated on 23 April 2026 at 11:51 by the SBOM pipeline documentation generator._
